items.title: VI Live: De Lange slachtoffer van Dembélé-show in Le Classique
items.summary: Zondag staat ons een fraaie voetbaldag te wachten. De duels van Feyenoord, Ajax en PSV volgen we in aparte liveblogs, maar in deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van alle andere duels. Bijvoorbeeld van de Engelse topper tussen Liverpool en Manchester City.

items.title: Guardiola looft Bernardo Silva: 'Een van de besten die ik heb getraind'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola was na het gewonnen duel met Liverpool vol lof over aanvoerder en doelpuntenmaker Bernardo Silva. De Spaanse manager deelde ook complimenten uit aan Dominik Szoboszlai.

items.title: 'Killjoy' - did VAR get late drama in Liverpool v Man City right?
items.summary: Manchester City's win at Liverpool ended in complete chaos - but did the referee and the VAR get Dominik Szoboszlai's red card right?

items.title: Pep warns Arsenal: 'A lot can happen' in title race
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has warned Arsenal that 'a lot can happen in 13 games' as the Premier League title race heats up.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: Bucks' Antetokounmpo invests in Chelsea Women
items.summary: NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo has announced he has joined the ownership group of Chelsea Women which includes his "friend" Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian.

items.title: Haaland earns City dramatic late win at Liverpool
items.summary: Erling Haaland kept Manchester City in the title race with an added-time penalty as Pep Guardiola's side came from behind to win 2-1 in a chaotic finish.

items.title: Foxborough demands clarity on $8M WC funding
items.summary: Town officials in Foxborough, Massachusetts, are seeking clarity on the source of around $8 million they say is needed to fund public safety for this summer's World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, the home of the New England Patriots.

items.title: Simonis in gesprek met Anderlecht, tweede gesprek volgt
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Man City produce late comeback to beat Liverpool in chaotic finale
items.summary: Erling Haaland's 93rd-minute penalty gives Manchester City a crucial comeback win over Liverpool, who end the game with 10 men as Dominik Szoboszlai is sent off in stoppage time at Anfield.

items.title: Crystal Palace end winless run with victory over rivals Brighton
items.summary: Ismaila Sarr scores the only goal of the game as Crystal Palace end a run of nine Premier League games without a win, by beating rivals Brighton 1-0 at the AMEX Stadium.

items.title: 'A long way back' - Carra expects Liverpool to miss out on CL spots
items.summary: Jamie Carragher expects Liverpool to miss out on Champions League qualification through the Premier League.

items.title: Niek Schiks, de oud-veldspeler die als doelman zijn PSV-debuut maakte
items.summary: Blessures van Matej Kovar én Nick Olij maakten van de 22-jarige Niek Schiks, een echte PSV’er, opeens de eerste doelman van zijn club tijdens de lastige uitwedstrijd tegen FC Groningen ( 1-2 ). De man die in 2012 de jeugdopleiding binnenliep als veldspeler, debuteerde verdienstelijk als keeper.

items.title: Slot haalt uit naar arbitrage: 'Scheidsrechter moet zijn werk doen'
items.summary: Arne Slot heeft na de verloren topper tegen Manchester City flink uitgehaald naar scheidsrechter Craig Pawson. De 46-jarige Engelsman besloot Marc Guéhi geen rode kaart te geven na een overtreding op Mohamed Salah.

items.title: Greaves prepares for PDC ProTour with perfect Women's Series weekend
items.summary: Beau Greaves kicked off the 2026 Women's Series with a perfect weekend in Germany as she made it 113 wins on the spin and 17 consecutive titles.

items.title: Slot bemoans luck with referees in loss to Man City
items.summary: Liverpool manager Arne Slot says the club have had poor luck with referees this season, believing that Marc Guehi should have been sent off for a challenge on Mohamed Salah in their loss to Manchester City.

items.title: Haaland scores stoppage-time penalty to seal DRAMATIC win over Liverpool!
items.summary: Highlights from the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City.

items.title: Manchester City keep up title chase with late comeback win at Liverpool
items.summary: Pep Guardiola threw himself back into his seat in the dugout. The Manchester City manager had just witnessed a moment of sheer brilliance and, like everyone connected to his club, he had to fear the worst. Because this is how it has tended to go for him at Anfield. The blow had been administered by Dominic Szoboszlai, the stand-in Liverpool right-back, and it is worth dwelling on it for a moment – if only a moment because it would be rapidly overtaken by a bonkers finale. When Szoboszlai addressed a free-kick 30 yards out, City did not look overly concerned. They only put two men in their wall. Continue reading...

items.title: WSL roundup: Chelsea get back into the groove at Spurs as Madonna watches on
items.summary: Walsh and Thompson seal 2-0 success at Tottenham Liverpool and West Ham win to boost survival hopes Chelsea defeated Tottenham 2-0 to boost their faint hopes of retaining the Women’s Super League title, as Madonna watched from the stands at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Goals from Keira Walsh and Alyssa Thompson either side of half-time ended Chelsea’s run of back-to-back defeats and left Sonia Bompastor’s team nine points off the leaders Manchester City, who lost 1-0 against Arsenal at the Emirates Stadium earlier on Sunday. Continue reading...

items.title: Haaland wins it late for Man City at Liverpool amid stoppage-time chaos at Anfield
items.summary: Erling Haaland scored a stoppage-time penalty as Manchester City came from behind late on to beat Liverpool 2-1 at Anfield – and keep the title race alive.
